inst✝³ : InnerProductSpace ℝ E
inst✝² : FiniteDimensional ℝ E
inst✝¹ : MeasurableSpace E
inst✝ : BorelSpace E
L : StrongDual ℝ E
⊢ variance (⇑L) (@ProbabilityTheory.stdGaussianMulti E inst✝⁴ inst✝³ inst✝² inst✝¹) =
    ∑ i, L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) i) ^ 2

classical
  -- unfold the definition through the map and compute the variance on the product space
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, variance_map (f := fun x ↦ ∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) (g := (⇑L))]
  · -- compute mean and second moment coordinatewise
    have hcenter : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
    rw [variance_def, hcenter, sub_zero]
    -- push integral back to the product measure
    simp only [Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop)] at hcenter
    rw [integral_map _ (by fun_prop)]
    swap
    ·
      exact
        ((Continuous.aemeasurable
              (by
                fun_prop :
                  Continuous fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
                    L (∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)))).pow_const
    -- expand L of the sum
    simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, LinearMap.map_sum, LinearMap.map_smulₛₗ,
      RingHom.id_apply]
    -- L (x i • e i) = x i * L(e i)
    have hL :
        (fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
            (L (∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2) =
          fun x =>
            (∑ i, (x i) * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2 := by
      funext x
      simp [mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
    simp [hL, pow_two, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul,
      Finset.sum_mul_sum]
    -- only diagonal terms remain since coordinates are independent and centered
    have hmean :
        ∀ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E),
          (∫ x, x i ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)) = 0 := by
      intro i
      have :
          (∫ (a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ), a i
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)) =
            ∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
        convert integral_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
            (f := id) ?_
        · rfl
        · infer_instance
        ·
          rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
          exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
      simpa [this] using (integral_id_gaussianReal (μ := gaussianReal 0 1) (m := (0 : ℝ)) (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
    have hsecond :
        ∀ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E),
          (∫ x, (x i) ^ 2 ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)) = 1 := by
      intro i
      have :
          (∫ (a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ), (a i) ^ 2
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)) =
            ∫ x, x ^ 2 ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
        convert integral_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
            (f := fun x ↦ x ^ 2) ?_
        · rfl
        · infer_instance
        ·
          -- integrability from Lp
          have : MemLp (fun x : ℝ => x ^ 2) 1 (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
            simpa [one_div] using (memLp_pow_gaussianReal (p := (1 : ℝ≥0∞)) (n := (2 : ℕ)) (m := (0 : ℝ))
              (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
          simpa [memLp_one_iff_integrable] using this
      -- variance of standard normal is 1 and mean is 0
      have hvar : Var[id; gaussianReal 0 1] = (1 : ℝ) := by
        simpa using (variance_id_gaussianReal (m := (0 : ℝ)) (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
      -- Var = E[X^2] - (E[X])^2, and E[X]=0
      have hE : (∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1) = (0 : ℝ) := by
        simpa using (integral_id_gaussianReal (μ := gaussianReal 0 1) (m := (0 : ℝ)) (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
      -- rewrite hvar
      have : (∫ x, x ^ 2 ∂gaussianReal 0 1) = (1 : ℝ) := by
        -- from variance definition
        rw [variance_def] at hvar
        simp [hE] at hvar
        simpa [pow_two, sub_eq_add_neg] using hvar
      simpa [this] using congrArg id (by simpa [this] using this)
    -- assemble: cross terms vanish, diagonal give sum of squares
    have :
        (∫ x,
            (∑ i, x i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)) =
          ∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) ^ 2 := by

    simpa [hL] using this
  ·

classical
  have hμ : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  rw [variance_eq_integral_sq_sub_mean, hμ]
  simp only [sub_zero]
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, integral_map _ (by fun_prop)]
  swap; · exact (Finset.measurable_sum _ (by fun_prop)).aemeasurable
  simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul]
  have h_expand :
      (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
        (∑ i, a i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2)
        =
      (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
        ∑ i, (a i) ^ 2 * (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2 +
          ∑ i in (Finset.univ.product Finset.univ).filter (fun ij : _ × _ => ij.1 ≠ ij.2),
            a ij.1 * a ij.2 * (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E ij.1)) * (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E ij.2))) := by
    funext a
    classical
    simp [sq, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.sum_mul_sum,
      Finset.sum_product, Finset.sum_filter, Finset.sum_add_distrib, add_mul, mul_add, pow_two,
      Finset.sum_sigma', Finset.sum_sigma, Finset.sum_product, Finset.filter_filter,
      Finset.sum_filter, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul]
  -- Use orthonormality/independence: cross terms integrate to 0, diagonal terms integrate to 1
  -- and the diagonal coefficients are exactly the claimed sum.
  -- Mathlib lemma already provides the variance for this construction.
  simpa [stdGaussianMulti] using
    (variance_dual_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L)

classical
  -- reduce to variance of a linear combination of independent standard Gaussians
  have hμprob :
      IsProbabilityMeasure (Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
    infer_instance
  have hcenter : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  -- compute `L` of the mapped vector
  have hmap :
      (fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
          L (∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) =
        fun x => ∑ i, (x i) * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) := by
    funext x
    simp [Finset.sum_mul]
  -- use the corresponding lemma for product standard Gaussians
  -- (available in `Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Gaussian.Basic`)
  simpa [stdGaussianMulti, Var, variance, hcenter, hmap, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc, pow_two]
    using variance_finset_sum_mul_gaussianReal (L := fun i => L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i))

classical
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, variance_map _ (by fun_prop)]
  · simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul]
    rw [variance_finset_sum]
    · have hcenter :
        ∀ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E),
          (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a i ∂Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) = 0 := by
          intro i
          have :
              (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a i ∂Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
                = ∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
              convert integral_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) ?_
              · rfl
              · infer_instance
              · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
                exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
          simp [this]
      have hvar :
        ∀ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E),
          variance (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ (L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) i)) * a i)
              (Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
            = (L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) i)) ^ 2 := by
          intro i
          have hint :
              Integrable (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i)
                (Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
            convert integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) ?_
            · infer_instance
            · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
              exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
          have hmean :
              (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, (L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) i)) * a i
                    ∂Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) = 0 := by
            simp [integral_mul_const, hcenter i]
          have :
              variance (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ (L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) i)) * a i)
                  (Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
                =
              (L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) i)) ^ 2 *
                variance (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i)
                  (Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
            simpa [variance_mul_const] 
          -- now compute the remaining variance of the coordinate under the product measure
          have hcoord :
              variance (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i)
                  (Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) = 1 := by
            -- reduce to gaussianReal
            have :
                variance (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i)
                    (Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
                  = variance (fun x : ℝ ↦ x) (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
              -- variance depends only on the law; use integral_eval_pi twice
              simp [variance, hcenter i, integral_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
                (f := fun x ↦ x ^ 2)
                (by
                  rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
                  exact (memLp_pow_id_gaussianReal 1 2))]
            -- variance of standard Gaussian is 1
            simpa [variance, gaussianReal, Real.sqrt_sq_eq_abs] using this
          -- finish
          simpa [this, hcoord, pow_two, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm] 
        -- sum of variances, cross terms vanish by independence/centering
        simp [hvar]
    · intro i hi
      refine Integrable.mul_const ?_ _
      convert integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) ?_
      · infer_instance
      · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
        exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
    · intro i hi j hj hij
      have h0 :
          (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ,
              ((L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) i)) * a i) *
                ((L ((stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E) j)) * a j)
              ∂Measure.pi fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) = 0 := by
        -- one factor has mean zero and coordinates are independent
        simp [integral_mul_const, integral_eval_pi, hij]
      simp [covariance, h0, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E)]
  · exact (Finset.measurable_sum _ (by fun_prop)).aemeasurable

classical
  -- unfold variance of a real-valued random variable under a measure
  simp [ProbabilityTheory.variance, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L, stdGaussianMulti]

classical
  rw [variance, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L]
  have hmeas :
      AEStronglyMeasurable (fun x : E ↦ (L x) ^ 2) (stdGaussianMulti E) := by
    have hcont : Continuous fun x : E ↦ (L x) ^ 2 :=
      (L.continuous).pow 2
    exact hcont.aestronglyMeasurable
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, integral_map _ (by fun_prop)]
  swap
  · exact hmeas
  simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul]
  have :
      (fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦
          (L (∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2) =
        fun x ↦
          (∑ i, (x i) * (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i))) ^ 2 := by
    funext x
    simp [Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
  rw [this]
  have hId : Integrable (fun x : ℝ ↦ x) (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
    rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
    exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
  have hmul (i j : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E)) :
      Integrable (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i * a j)
        (Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
    by_cases hij : i = j
    · subst hij
      have hsq : Integrable (fun x : ℝ ↦ x ^ 2) (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
        simpa [pow_two] using (integrable_mul hId hId)
      simpa [pow_two, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm] using
        (integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := fun x ↦ x ^ 2) hsq)
    ·
      have hi : Integrable (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i)
          (Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
        simpa using
          (integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hId)
      have hj : Integrable (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a j)
          (Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
        simpa using
          (integrable_eval_pi (i := j) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hId)
      simpa [mul_comm] using (hi.mul hj)
  have hcov (i j : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E)) :
      (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a i * a j
            ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) =
        (if i = j then 1 else 0) := by
    by_cases hij : i = j
    · subst hij
      have :
          (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, (a i) ^ 2
              ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) =
            ∫ x, x ^ 2 ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
        convert integral_eval_pi (i := i)
            (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
            (f := fun x ↦ x ^ 2) ?_
        · rfl
        · infer_instance
        ·
          have hsq : Integrable (fun x : ℝ ↦ x ^ 2) (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
            simpa [pow_two] using (integrable_mul hId hId)
          exact hsq
      have hvar : (∫ x, x ^ 2 ∂gaussianReal 0 1) = (1 : ℝ) := by
        simpa [pow_two] using (integral_mul_self_gaussianReal (μ := gaussianReal 0 1))
      simpa [if_pos rfl, pow_two] using (by simpa [this] using congrArg id hvar)
    ·
      have hi : Integrable (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i)
          (Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
        simpa using
          (integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hId)
      have hj : Integrable (fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a j)
          (Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
        simpa using
          (integrable_eval_pi (i := j) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hId)
      have hEi :
          (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a i
              ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) = 0 := by
        have :
            (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a i
                ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) =
              ∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
          convert integral_eval_pi (i := i)
              (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hId <;> rfl
        simpa [this] using (integral_id_gaussianReal (μ := gaussianReal 0 1))
      have hEj :
          (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a j
              ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) = 0 := by
        have :
            (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a j
                ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) =
              ∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
          convert integral_eval_pi (i := j)
              (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hId <;> rfl
        simpa [this] using (integral_id_gaussianReal (μ := gaussianReal 0 1))
      have hprod :
          (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a i * a j
                ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) =
            (∫ a, a i ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) *
              (∫ a, a j ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) := by
        simpa [mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc] using
          (integral_mul_prod_mk (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)
            (i := i) (j := j) hij hi hj)
      simp [hcov, hij, hprod, hEi, hEj, if_neg hij]
  classical
  have :
      (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ,
          (∑ i, x i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2
            ∂Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) =
        ∑ i, (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2 := by
    classical
    simp [pow_two, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, hcov, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
  simpa [Var, variance, this]

classical
  -- unfold variance as second moment since the mean is zero
  have hcenter : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  simp [ProbabilityTheory.variance, hcenter, stdGaussianMulti]

classical
  -- `stdGaussianMulti` is the image of an i.i.d. standard Gaussian family under a linear map;
  -- compute variance using orthonormality and independence.
  have hmean : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  rw [ProbabilityTheory.variance, hmean]
  -- unfold `stdGaussianMulti` and push the integral back to the product space
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, integral_map _ (by fun_prop)]
  swap
  ·
    -- measurability of the squared function of the finite sum
    have hm :
        AEMeasurable
            (fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
              (L (∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2)
            (Measure.pi fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1) := by
      fun_prop
    exact hm
  -- simplify the map
  simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, LinearMap.map_sum, LinearMap.map_smulₛₗ,
    ContinuousLinearMap.map_sum, ContinuousLinearMap.map_smulₛₗ]
  -- rewrite the square of a sum as a double sum and use independence / centeredness
  have hL :
      (fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
          (L (∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2) =
        (fun x =>
          (∑ i, (x i) * (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i))) ^ 2) := by
    funext x
    simp [Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
  simp only [hL]
  -- expand square into double sum
  rw [pow_two, Finset.mul_sum, integral_finset_sum]
  swap
  · intro i hi
    refine Integrable.mul_const ?_ _
    convert integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
        (f := id) ?_
    · infer_instance
    · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
      exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
  -- inner sum
  rw [Finset.sum_mul, integral_finset_sum]
  swap
  · intro j hj
    refine Integrable.mul_const ?_ _
    convert integrable_eval_pi (i := j) (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
        (f := id) ?_
    · infer_instance
    · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
      exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
  -- evaluate expectations; cross terms vanish, diagonal terms give variance 1
  have hdiag :
      (fun i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) =>
          ∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, (x i) ^ 2
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) =
        fun _ => (1 : ℝ) := by
    funext i
    have :
        ∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, (x i) ^ 2
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
          = ∫ t : ℝ, t ^ 2 ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
      convert integral_eval_pi (i := i)
          (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
          (f := fun t => t ^ 2) ?_
      · rfl
      · infer_instance
      ·
        -- integrable square under standard Gaussian
        simpa [pow_two] using (memLp_mul_id_gaussianReal (p := (1 : ℝ≥0∞))).integrable
    -- standard Gaussian second moment is 1
    simpa [this] using (integral_sq_gaussianReal (m := (0 : ℝ)) (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
  -- use independence: for i ≠ j, E[x_i x_j] = 0
  have hcross :
      ∀ i j : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E),
        i ≠ j →
          (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, (x i) * (x j)
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) = 0 := by
    intro i j hij
    -- use product structure: factor expectation into product of means
    have hi :
        ∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x i
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
          = (0 : ℝ) := by
      have :
          ∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x i
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
            = ∫ t : ℝ, t ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
        convert integral_eval_pi (i := i)
            (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
            (f := id) ?_
        · rfl
        · infer_instance
        · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
          exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
      simpa [this] using (integral_id_gaussianReal (m := (0 : ℝ)) (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
    have hj :
        ∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x j
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
          = (0 : ℝ) := by
      have :
          ∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x j
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
            = ∫ t : ℝ, t ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
        convert integral_eval_pi (i := j)
            (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
            (f := id) ?_
        · rfl
        · infer_instance
        · rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
          exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
      simpa [this] using (integral_id_gaussianReal (m := (0 : ℝ)) (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
    -- now compute mixed moment as product (independence) and conclude
    simpa [hi, hj, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm] using
      (integral_mul_eval_pi_eq_mul_integral_eval_pi (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) =>
        gaussianReal 0 1) hij)
  -- assemble result
  -- after expanding: sum over i=j gives (L e_i)^2 * 1, cross terms 0
  have :
      (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ,
          (∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), x i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) *
              (∑ j : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), x j * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E j))
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1))
        =
        ∑ i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E), (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2 := by
    -- expand and evaluate integrals termwise
    simp [Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, pow_two, hcross, hdiag, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
  simpa [pow_two] using this

classical
  rw [stdGaussianMulti]
  -- reduce to variance of a linear image of an i.i.d. standard Gaussian vector
  simp only [ProbabilityTheory.variance, ProbabilityTheory.Var, Measure.map_apply (by fun_prop)]
  -- `simp` knows the variance of a finite sum of independent centered gaussians, and
  -- the coordinates are independent under `Measure.pi`.
  simpa using (variance_dual_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L)

classical
  have hmean : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  rw [variance_def]
  simp [hmean, stdGaussianMulti]
  -- reduce to variance of a sum of independent standard Gaussians along an orthonormal basis
  have hμ :
      Var[L; stdGaussianMulti E] =
        Var[(fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
            ∑ i, x i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i));
          Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)] := by
    simp [stdGaussianMulti, Var, variance, variance_def, integral_map, (by fun_prop : Measurable _)]
  -- use the standard computation: variance of linear combination of independent N(0,1)
  -- is sum of squares of coefficients
  -- Mathlib lemma: `variance_sum_mul_gaussianReal_pi` (in `Mathlib.Probability.Distributions.Gaussian.Basic`)
  simpa [hμ, mul_comm, mul_left_comm, mul_assoc, pow_two] using
    variance_sum_mul_gaussianReal_pi (c := fun i => L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i))
simpa [stdGaussianMulti]

classical
  -- identify with the standard Gaussian on `Fin (finrank ℝ E) → ℝ` mapped through the ONB
  simp [stdGaussianMulti, ProbabilityTheory.variance, ProbabilityTheory.Var, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L]

classical
  rw [variance_eq_integral_sq_sub]
  simp [isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L, stdGaussianMulti, integral_map, map_sum, map_smul,
    smul_eq_mul, integral_finset_sum, integrable_eval_pi, integral_eval_pi]

classical
  rw [ProbabilityTheory.variance, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L]
  simp [stdGaussianMulti, integral_map, map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, pow_two]

classical
  -- `Var[L; μ] = ∫ x, (L x) ^ 2 ∂μ` when `μ` is centered for `L`
  have hcenter : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  simp [ProbabilityTheory.variance, hcenter, stdGaussianMulti, integral_map, (by fun_prop),
    Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]

classical
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, variance_map]
  swap
  · exact (Finset.measurable_sum _ (by fun_prop)).aemeasurable
  simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul, PiLp.inner_apply, LinearMap.coe_coe]
  rw [variance_finset_sum]
  · refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
    intro i hi
    simp only [variance_mul_const]
    have hVar :
        Var[(fun a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ ↦ a i);
            Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1)]
          = Var[id; gaussianReal 0 1] := by
      simpa using (variance_eval_pi (i := i)
        (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1))
    simp [hVar]
  · intro i hi
    refine (integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) ?_)
    rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
    exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
  · intro i hi
    refine (integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ ↦ gaussianReal 0 1) (f := fun x ↦ x ^ 2) ?_)
    rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
    simpa [pow_two] using (memLp_mul_id_gaussianReal 1)

classical
  simp [ProbabilityTheory.stdGaussianMulti, variance, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L]

classical
  -- identify `stdGaussianMulti E` as the pushforward of an i.i.d. standard normal vector
  simp [ProbabilityTheory.stdGaussianMulti, variance_map, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E)]

classical
  rw [variance_eq_integral_sq_sub]
  have hcenter : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  simp [hcenter, stdGaussianMulti, Measure.integral_map (by fun_prop),
    Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm,
    sub_eq_add_neg, add_assoc, add_left_comm, add_comm]

classical
  simp [stdGaussianMulti, ProbabilityTheory.stdGaussianMulti, variance, isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L]

classical
  -- `stdGaussianMulti E` is the pushforward of an i.i.d. standard Gaussian vector by a linear map.
  -- The variance of `L` under the pushforward is the variance of the corresponding linear form.
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, ProbabilityTheory.variance_map (μ := Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ gaussianReal 0 1))
      (f := fun x ↦ ∑ i, x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) (g := fun y ↦ L y)]
  · simp only [StrongDual.coe_coe, map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul]
    -- expand the variance of a sum of independent centered Gaussians
    simpa using
      variance_sum_mul_eval_stdGaussianReal
        (L := fun i : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) ↦ L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i))
  · exact (Finset.measurable_sum _ (by fun_prop)).aemeasurable

rw [stdGaussianMulti, variance_map _ (by fun_prop)]
  swap
  · exact (Finset.measurable_sum _ (by fun_prop)).aemeasurable
  simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul]
  have hmean : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  rw [variance_eq_integral_sq_sub_mean, hmean, sub_zero]
  simp only [StrongDual.coe_coe, Finset.sum_apply, LinearMap.map_sum, LinearMap.map_smul,
    smul_eq_mul]
  have hL :
      (fun a : (Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ) =>
          L (∑ i, a i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) =
        fun a => ∑ i, a i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) := by
    funext a
    simp [Finset.mul_sum]
  simp [hL, integral_finset_sum]
  classical
  have hIndep :
      Pairwise fun i j : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) =>
        i ≠ j →
          (∫ a, (a i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) *
                (a j * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E j))
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) = 0 := by
    intro i j hij
    have hi :
        Integrable (fun x : ℝ => x) (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
      rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
      exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
    have hj : Integrable (fun x : ℝ => x) (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
      rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
      exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
    have hEi :
        (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a i
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) =
          (∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1) := by
      simpa using (integral_eval_pi (d := Module.finrank ℝ E) (i := i)
        (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hi)
    have hEj :
        (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, a j
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) =
          (∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1) := by
      simpa using (integral_eval_pi (d := Module.finrank ℝ E) (i := j)
        (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1) (f := id) hj)
    have h0 : (∫ x, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1) = 0 := by
      simpa using (integral_id_gaussianReal (m := (0 : ℝ)) (σ := (1 : ℝ)))
    have hprod :
        (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, (a i) * (a j)
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) = 0 := by
      -- use independence of coordinates in product measure
      simpa [mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm, hEi, hEj, h0] using
        (integral_mul_eval_pi_eq_mul_integral_eval_pi (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) =>
          gaussianReal 0 1) hij (f := id) (g := id) hi hj)
    simp [mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm, hprod]
  have hsquare :
      (∫ a : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ,
          (∑ i, a i * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2
        ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) =
        ∑ i, (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2 := by
    -- expand square and kill cross terms
    simp [pow_two, mul_add, add_mul, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul, Finset.sum_add_distrib,
      Finset.sum_sigma', Finset.sum_product, Finset.sum_comm, Finset.sum_left_comm,
      integral_finset_sum, hIndep, integral_mul_const, integral_const_mul, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm,
      mul_comm, integral_eval_pi, integrable_eval_pi, memLp_id_gaussianReal, memLp_one_iff_integrable,
      integral_sq_gaussianReal, pow_two]
  simpa [variance, hsquare]

classical
  -- `stdGaussianMulti` is the pushforward of an i.i.d. standard Gaussian vector along the linear map
  -- `x ↦ ∑ i, x i • e i`. Compute the variance by expanding the square and using independence/centering.
  have hcenter : (stdGaussianMulti E)[L] = 0 := isCentered_stdGaussianMulti (E := E) L
  rw [Var, hcenter, sub_zero]
  rw [stdGaussianMulti, integral_map _ (by fun_prop)]
  swap
  ·
    -- aemeasurable of the integrand
    refine (continuous_finset_sum _ ?_).aemeasurable
    intro i hi
    -- continuity of `x ↦ (L (x i • e i))^2`
    have : Continuous fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
        (L (x i • stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2 := by
      fun_prop
    simpa using this
  -- expand the map and simplify
  simp only [map_sum, map_smul, smul_eq_mul]
  -- rewrite the integrand
  have hlin :
      (fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
          (∑ i, (x i) * L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2) =
        fun x =>
          (∑ i, (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) * x i) ^ 2 := by
    funext x
    refine congrArg (fun t => t ^ 2) ?_
    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl ?_
    intro i hi
    ring
  -- use bilinearity of square: (∑ a_i)^2 = ∑_{i,j} a_i a_j
  -- then integrate termwise; cross terms vanish by centering; diagonal terms give variance 1.
  -- We implement via `Finset.sum_mul_sum` and `integral_finset_sum`.
  rw [hlin]
  simp only [pow_two]
  -- turn the product of sums into double sum
  have :
      (fun x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ =>
          (∑ i, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) * x i) *
            (∑ j, L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E j) * x j)) =
        fun x =>
          ∑ i, ∑ j, (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) * x i) *
            (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E j) * x j) := by
    funext x
    simpa [Finset.sum_mul_sum, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul]
  rw [this]
  -- integrate the double sum
  rw [integral_finset_sum]
  swap
  · intro i hi
    refine Integrable.finset_sum ?_
    intro j hj
    -- integrable of the product
    refine (Integrable.mul ?_ ?_)
    ·
      refine Integrable.mul_const ?_ _
      convert integrable_eval_pi (i := i) (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
          (f := id) ?_ using 1
      · rfl
      · infer_instance
      ·
        rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
        exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
    ·
      refine Integrable.mul_const ?_ _
      convert integrable_eval_pi (i := j) (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
          (f := id) ?_ using 1
      · rfl
      · infer_instance
      ·
        rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
        exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
  -- compute each term: diagonal gives coefficient^2, off-diagonal is 0
  have hE :
      ∀ i j : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E),
        (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ,
            (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i) * x i) *
              (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E j) * x j)
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)) =
          (if i = j then (L (stdOrthonormalBasis ℝ E i)) ^ 2 else 0) := by
    intro i j
    by_cases hij : i = j
    · subst hij
      -- diagonal: coefficient^2 * E[X^2] with X~N(0,1), E[X^2]=1
      simp [mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm, pow_two]
      -- reduce to integral of x^2 under gaussianReal
      have hproj :
          (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, (x i) ^ 2
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1))
            = ∫ x : ℝ, x ^ 2 ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
        convert integral_eval_pi (i := i)
            (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
            (f := fun x : ℝ => x ^ 2) ?_ using 1
        · rfl
        · infer_instance
        ·
          -- integrable of x^2 under standard Gaussian
          have : MemLp (fun x : ℝ => x) 2 (gaussianReal 0 1) := memLp_id_gaussianReal 2
          have h2 : Integrable (fun x : ℝ => x ^ 2) (gaussianReal 0 1) := by
            -- use `memLp` to get integrable of square
            simpa [pow_two] using (this.mul this).integrable
          exact h2
      -- use known second moment of standard Gaussian
      -- `gaussianReal 0 1` has variance 1 and mean 0, so E[X^2]=1
      have hmean : (∫ x : ℝ, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1) = 0 := by simp
      have hvar : Var[id; gaussianReal 0 1] = 1 := by simp
      have hsecond :
          (∫ x : ℝ, x ^ 2 ∂gaussianReal 0 1) = 1 := by
        -- Var = E[X^2] - (E[X])^2 for centered
        simpa [Var, hmean, pow_two] using hvar
      -- finish
      simp [hproj, hsecond, pow_two, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm]
    ·
      -- off-diagonal: independent centered coordinates => expectation 0
      have hi :
          (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x i
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1))
            = 0 := by
        have : (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x i
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1))
            = ∫ x : ℝ, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
          convert integral_eval_pi (i := i)
              (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
              (f := id) ?_ using 1
          · rfl
          · infer_instance
          ·
            rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
            exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
        simp [this]
      have hj :
          (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x j
              ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1))
            = 0 := by
        have : (∫ x : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) → ℝ, x j
            ∂Measure.pi (fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1))
            = ∫ x : ℝ, x ∂gaussianReal 0 1 := by
          convert integral_eval_pi (i := j)
              (μ := fun _ : Fin (Module.finrank ℝ E) => gaussianReal 0 1)
              (f := id) ?_ using 1
          · rfl
          · infer_instance
          ·
            rw [← memLp_one_iff_integrable]
            exact memLp_id_gaussianReal 1
        simp [this]
      -- use independence of different coordinates in product measure: E[x_i*x_j]=E[x_i]*E[x_j]=0
      -- and pull out constants
      simp [hij, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm, mul_comm, hi, hj]
  -- sum over i,j using hE; only diagonal remains
  simp [hE, Finset.sum_sigma', Finset.sum_ite_irrel, Finset.sum_ite_eq, Finset.sum_const_zero,
    Finset.sum_mul, Finset.mul_sum, Finset.sum_mul_sum, pow_two]
